steps.title: Spot the reversal
steps.content: The passage establishes that former Spanish colonies still speak Spanish (Cuba example). Then it signals we're shifting to the Netherlands, which also left the Spanish Empire but — unlike Cuba — Spanish is “rarely spoken” there. The transition must signal this opposite outcome.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
”The reverse is true of the Netherlands” explicitly signals a flip from one case to its opposite.

Choices B, A, and D either treat the Netherlands as fitting the original pattern, awkwardly name rather than show the contrast, or cloud the relationship with vague language.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
